mcqs.question: Which communication protocol is most suitable for soil moisture sensors scattered across a large farm due to its long-range and low-power characteristics?

options.text: Wi-Fi
Bluetooth
LoRaWAN
Ethernet

mcqs.explanation: LoRaWAN is designed for long-range communication (several kilometers) with very low power consumption, making it ideal for agricultural IoT applications where devices are spread out and need to operate on batteries for long periods.

mcqs.question: What is the primary purpose of soil moisture sensors in a precision agriculture system?

options.text: To measure the pH level of the soil
To determine the water content in the soil for optimizing irrigation
To detect the presence of pests in the field
To measure the temperature of the soil surface

mcqs.explanation: Soil moisture sensors measure the volumetric water content in the soil. This data is critical for automated irrigation systems to determine when and how much water to apply, preventing both over-watering (waste) and under-watering (crop stress).

mcqs.question: In an IoT-based precision agriculture system, what is the typical role of a Raspberry Pi?

options.text: It acts solely as a soil moisture sensor
It functions as a long-range base station for LoRa communication only
It serves as a gateway, aggregating sensor data and forwarding it to the cloud
It is used exclusively for cloud-based data analytics

mcqs.explanation: In precision agriculture, the Raspberry Pi typically serves as a gateway device. It aggregates data from multiple field sensors, performs local pre-processing, and forwards data to the cloud via WiFi or cellular connection.

mcqs.question: Which of the following best describes the logical data flow in an IoT precision agriculture system?

options.text: Actuator -> Sensor -> Cloud -> User
Sensor -> Gateway -> Cloud Analytics -> Actuator
User -> Actuator -> Sensor -> Cloud
Cloud -> Sensor -> Actuator -> Analytics

mcqs.explanation: The standard flow begins with sensors generating data in the field. The gateway aggregates and forwards this data to the cloud. Cloud analytics processes the data, and if action is needed (e.g., soil is too dry), a command is sent back to an actuator (e.g., water valve) to perform an action.

mcqs.question: What is the main advantage of using NDVI (Normalized Difference Vegetation Index) from drone imagery in smart agriculture?

options.text: It directly measures soil nutrient levels
It assesses crop health and identifies stressed vegetation areas
It controls the irrigation valves automatically
It provides weather forecasting data

mcqs.explanation: NDVI uses multispectral camera data to measure vegetation health. Healthy plants reflect more near-infrared light and absorb more visible red light. Low NDVI values indicate stressed or unhealthy crops, enabling targeted interventions.

mcqs.question: Which challenge is most critical when deploying IoT sensors in remote agricultural fields?

options.text: High-speed internet connectivity
Power supply and energy harvesting for long-term operation
Real-time video streaming capability
Touchscreen interfaces for farmer interaction

mcqs.explanation: Remote agricultural fields often lack electricity access. IoT sensors must operate on batteries or solar panels for months or years. Low-power design, sleep modes, and energy harvesting are critical for sustained field deployment.

mcqs.question: A farm deploys 500 soil sensors across 100 acres, each sending readings every 15 minutes via LoRaWAN to a central gateway. The gateway processes data locally and forwards daily summaries to the cloud. What is the primary benefit of this edge-processing approach?

options.text: It eliminates the need for any cloud infrastructure
It reduces cellular bandwidth costs and enables faster local irrigation decisions
It provides better visualization dashboards than cloud solutions
It makes the sensors last longer by reducing their transmission frequency

mcqs.explanation: Edge processing at the gateway reduces the amount of data sent to the cloud (only daily summaries instead of raw readings), lowering cellular bandwidth costs. It also enables faster local decisions for time-critical irrigation control without waiting for cloud round-trip.

mcqs.question: An IoT agriculture system needs to detect frost conditions and activate greenhouse heaters within 30 seconds. Which architecture component is most important for meeting this latency requirement?

options.text: Cloud analytics platform with machine learning
Edge gateway with local decision-making capability
Long-range LoRaWAN communication
Historical data storage in a time-series database

mcqs.explanation: For time-critical responses like frost protection, the edge gateway must process temperature data locally and trigger actuators directly. Cloud round-trip latency (seconds to minutes) would exceed the 30-second requirement. The edge gateway can execute pre-programmed rules instantly.
